sources.notes: Construct a length-8 distance-convex sequence with at most 6 distinct values.
Hard-version statement gives the same distance-convex definition and the example construction [1,1,3,6,10,3,11,1].

annotation.summary: Output any length-8 sequence using at most 6 distinct values whose consecutive jump lengths are strictly increasing.
annotation.constraints: The only easy test is fixed at n=8,m=6, so a hardcoded valid construction is acceptable.
annotation.core_idea: Use the sample-style sequence [1,1,3,6,10,3,11,1]. Its consecutive distances are 0,2,3,4,7,8,10, which are strictly increasing, and it uses only five distinct values. This satisfies the fixed easy test immediately.

solution_variants.name: hardcoded-sample-construction
solution_variants.summary: Print a known valid length-8 distance-convex sequence.

tags.evidence: The accepted solution can simply output an explicit valid sequence.
Correctness is verified by arithmetic inequalities on absolute differences.
